title: Bridge Essay: A voice without a mouth no more: The neurobiology of language and consciousness.
concept: Mantra meditation, inner speech, focused attention, language as mantra.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Language/Mantra** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Language as mantra — mantra meditation, inner speech, focused attention, verbal repetition.
**Phase context:** Mantra meditation, inner speech, focused attention, language as mantra.

**A voice without a mouth no more: The neurobiology of language and consciousness.** — Neurosci Biobehav Rev (2022). DOI: 10.1016/j.neubiorev.2022.104772. PMID: 35835286. Most research on the neurobiology of language ignores consciousness and vice versa. Here, language, with an emphasis on inner speech, is hypothesised to generate and sustain self-awareness, i.e., higher-order consciousness. Converging evidence supporting this hypothesis is reviewed. To account for these findings, a 'HOLISTIC' model of neurobiology of language, inner speech, and consciousness is proposed. It involves a 'core' set of inner speech production regions that initiate the experience of
